description: Clean baseline for the solver3 objective autoresearch lane after the x100 metric-scale reset; per-case score metrics are the main anti-gaming signal.

Adaptive temperature calibration: catastrophically bad. Calibrated T_initial too high (~55 for balance cases), making search random. Also surfaced deeper bug: cooling schedule uses max_iterations=3M but only ~188K iterations complete in 15s, so temperature barely changes.

asi.hypothesis: Calibrate T_initial from positive delta samples to give 40% uphill acceptance
asi.rollback_reason: Primary 186.62 vs baseline 107.85. T_initial calibrated to ~55, T_final=1.375 → search never converges.
asi.root_cause_discovered: Cooling schedule mismatch: max_iterations=3M but only ~188K iterations in 15s → temperature stays near 2.0 throughout. T_initial=2.0 too low for balance penalties but adaptive calibration overshoots.
asi.next_action_hint: Switch to TIME-BASED cooling (elapsed/time_limit as progress) when time_limit is set. Keep T_initial=2.0, T_final=0.05 but let cooling happen in wall-clock space. This alone won't help balance case but fixes the cooling mismatch first.

description: Time-based cooling: max(iter_fraction, time_fraction) as SA progress. Fixes the schedule mismatch where max_iterations=3M but only ~188K iters complete in 15s. attribute_balance 1.404→0.995 (beats reference), synthetic_partial below reference too. Also 3x raw iteration speedup from debouncing Instant::elapsed every 64 iters.

asi.hypothesis: max(iter_progress, time_progress) drives SA cooling so whichever stop-condition binds ensures T reaches T_final
asi.key_finding: Iteration rate ~188K/15s vs max_iterations=3M meant T barely moved (stayed at 1.64 throughout). With time-fraction dominating, T now properly cools 2.0→0.05 over the budget. attribute_balance 1.404→0.995, primary 107.85→100.11
asi.speedup: Debouncing Instant::elapsed every 64 iters cut solver3_raw_score_us from 79.6 to 24.7 (3x)
asi.remaining_gaps: large_gender 1.015x, sailing_trip 1.017x — both within 2% of reference

description: T_initial=1.0, T_final=0.02: cooler start hurt sailing_trip (1.017→1.034) and large_gender (1.015→1.025). Sailing fixed-iteration also regressed to 1.089, confirming T=1.0 is too cold for cases that need exploration. Previous T=2.0 was better.

asi.hypothesis: T_initial=1.0, T_final=0.02 reduces random hot-phase exploration
asi.rollback_reason: 100.17 vs prev 100.11. sailing_trip 1.017→1.034, large_gender 1.015→1.025. Fixed-iteration sailing 1.089 confirms too cold. Cases needing escape from local optima need T≥2.0.
asi.next_action_hint: T_initial=2.0, T_final=0.05 is the best known. Try population-based restart or reheating when no_improvement > threshold to improve sailing/gender gaps within the existing T schedule.

description: Two-cycle incumbent restart (midpoint warm restart from best-so-far) regressed overall. It helped sailing_trip (1.017→0.992) but hurt attribute_balance (0.995→1.045) and fixed-iteration quality. The restart family is still promising, but unconditional midpoint restart is too disruptive.

asi.hypothesis: A second full cooling cycle from the incumbent best state can escape late local minima better than one long SA pass
asi.rollback_reason: Primary regressed 100.11→100.95. Attribute-balance and fixed-iteration quality both worsened materially. Unconditional midpoint restart is too blunt.
asi.mechanism_family_signal: Still promising as a restart/perturbation family because sailing_trip improved below reference while other cases regressed. Needs selective triggering, not mandatory midpoint restart on every case.
asi.next_action_hint: Incubation follow-up: make restart conditional on stagnation/no-improvement and only when current state has drifted meaningfully from best. Avoid restarting cases already converging well.

description: Conditional stagnation restart is much better than unconditional midpoint restart: fixed-iteration recovered to 100.0 and sailing stayed below reference, but primary still regressed vs best (100.11→100.23) because attribute_balance slipped from 0.995 to 1.015. Restart family still plausible with stricter trigger.

asi.hypothesis: Restart from incumbent-best only under stagnation can help hard local-minimum cases without forcing all cases through a second cooling cycle
asi.rollback_reason: Still worse than best: 100.11→100.23, mainly due to slight attribute_balance regression. Restart trigger is still too permissive.
asi.mechanism_family_signal: Directionally better than unconditional restart (100.95→100.23), so the family is not dead yet. Sailing and synthetic remain good; stricter trigger may preserve attribute_balance.
asi.next_action_hint: Final incubation follow-up: require larger drift-from-best and later progress window before restart, so only truly stuck runs reheat. If that still misses, retire restart family for now and move to a new neighborhood/move type.

description: Final restart-family incubation attempt with much stricter trigger still regressed (100.60). It preserved attribute_balance/fixed-iteration but lost the sailing benefit and slowed hotpaths badly. Restart-based perturbation is retired for now; next experiments should pivot to a genuinely new neighborhood/move type.

asi.hypothesis: A very strict stagnation-triggered incumbent restart might preserve already-good cases while still helping rare local-minimum cases
asi.rollback_reason: Primary 100.60 vs best 100.11. Sailing benefit disappeared while hotpath and runtime diagnostics degraded materially. Restart-family ROI is poor.
asi.mechanism_family_signal: After three variants, restart/perturbation does not look like the next best path for this lane. It can be revisited later, but should be deprioritized now.
asi.next_action_hint: Pivot to a true search-innovation experiment: implement a new compound move / neighborhood rather than more restart tuning.

description: Tried a richer best-of-k sampled neighborhood inside existing move families (k=3 for swap/transfer, k=1 for clique). This enlarged the local neighborhood and helped sailing, but regressed the primary fixed-time aggregate versus the 100.105 best due to runtime inflation (~48us raw score vs ~24.7 baseline) and losses on large_gender/social_golfer/synthetic.

asi.hypothesis: A small best-of-k random neighborhood inside swap/transfer would convert recent speed headroom into better local move quality and improve the fixed-time solver3 objective suite.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric regressed from the best kept run and hotpath/runtime cost roughly doubled, so the richer neighborhood was too expensive at k=3.
asi.next_action_hint: Incubate the neighborhood family in a cheaper form: try asymmetric draws (e.g. swap=2, transfer=1), selective best-of-k only when temperature is low or for one family at a time, or targeted candidate generation rather than brute extra previews.
observations: Fixed-time objective suite score 100.2793 was worse than the best kept 100.1055.
Sailing improved to 1.062, but large_gender fell to 0.985 and social_golfer/synthetic also slipped.
solver3_raw_score_us increased to 48.04, suggesting preview multiplication dominated the benefit.

description: Incubated the richer-neighborhood family with a cheap asymmetric variant: best-of-2 on swap only, transfer/clique unchanged. This still regressed the primary metric badly, driven by a huge sailing collapse, so greedy local preview selection appears harmful for this family even when the runtime overhead is moderated.

asi.hypothesis: A cheaper swap-only best-of-k neighborhood might retain the upside of richer local choice without the runtime inflation of sampling multiple transfer previews.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric worsened sharply to 112.6265, with sailing exploding to 1.8003 fixed-time and 1.9072 fixed-iteration, so the neighborhood family appears directionally wrong rather than merely too expensive.
asi.next_action_hint: Retire naïve best-of-k preview selection for now. If revisiting neighborhood enrichment later, make it structurally different: target specific conflict patterns, add diversity/randomization guards, or score compound moves rather than greedily picking the locally best sampled swap.
observations: Swap-only best-of-2 still increased solver3_raw_score_us to 34.15, but the main failure was search quality, not just runtime.
The damage concentrated in stretch_sailing_trip_demo_real while most other cases stayed near baseline.
This suggests local delta-greediness can steer solver3 into a bad basin on sailing-like instances.

description: Tried a lightweight exact-undo tabu memory for swap and transfer moves, blocking recent reverse moves while leaving clique swaps unchanged. This was a genuine metaheuristic change, but it still regressed the primary fixed-time aggregate versus the 100.105 best and increased solver overhead materially.

asi.hypothesis: A tiny tabu memory that blocks immediate reverse swap/transfer moves would reduce cycling and let solver3 spend more time exploring new basins, improving fixed-time objective quality.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric worsened to 101.0031 and runtime overhead rose notably, so exact-undo tabu did not pay for itself.
asi.next_action_hint: Retire simple reverse-move tabu for now. If revisiting search memory later, make it more state-aware: target repeated group-pair churn or repeated person-pair contact regressions rather than blanket move reversal bans.
observations: Large_gender, sailing, and synthetic all improved over reference, but not enough to beat the best kept aggregate because social_golfer slipped and runtime cost increased.
solver3_raw_score_us rose to 39.82 and clique/transfer path timings also increased, so even lightweight policy checks had a noticeable search cost.
This suggests solver3's current bottleneck is not simple immediate move reversal cycling.

description: Replaced simulated annealing with Late Acceptance Hill Climbing using a rolling 256-score history as the acceptance reference. This was a true non-SA driver, but it badly destabilized transfer attribute balance and large_gender, so the pure LAHC policy is not suitable in this form for solver3's canonical fixed-time bundle.

asi.hypothesis: A late-acceptance driver would escape SA's temperature-shape biases and improve basin traversal under fixed-time runs with modest overhead.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric regressed sharply to 106.4029, with especially bad damage on transfer attribute balance and large_gender, indicating that pure late-acceptance acceptance is too permissive or misaligned for solver3's penalty landscape.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not pursue pure LAHC unchanged. If revisiting non-SA acceptance, try a guarded variant: threshold acceptance with a shrinking cap, record-to-record travel, or a hybrid gate that only allows uphill moves when they stay within bounded regression on constraint-heavy penalties.
observations: Attribute-balance case worsened to 1.2525 fixed-time and 1.2794 fixed-iteration.
Large_gender worsened materially while sailing slightly improved versus reference.
Runtime overhead also increased to solver3_raw_score_us 36.07, so the driver was neither better nor cheaper.
asi.mechanism_family: non-SA acceptance / late acceptance hill climbing

description: Incubated the adaptive move-family bandit with a cost-aware UCB score that penalized expensive families by average family time. This variant catastrophically regressed the primary metric, largely by blowing up sailing, so this cost-aware bandit formulation is decisively not a keep.

asi.hypothesis: A cost-aware UCB family selector would preserve the upside of adaptive move-family allocation while avoiding over-spending on expensive families such as clique previews.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric worsened dramatically to 115.6401 and sailing exploded to 1.9590, indicating that the cost-aware bandit pushed solver3 into a very bad search regime.
asi.next_action_hint: Retire this cost-aware UCB variant. If online family adaptation is revisited, use richer reward signals tied to best-score improvements or case-agnostic structural conflict reduction, not simple improving-accept counts with cost penalties.
observations: The previous plain UCB family selector was mildly bad; adding cost awareness made the family much worse rather than better.
solver3_raw_score_us ballooned to 68.34 despite the intent to control cost.
The failure concentrated in sailing, suggesting the selector starved or mis-sequenced the move families that keep that landscape stable.

name: Solver3 fixed-time objective-quality autoresearch (x100 scale) [new-machine baseline reset]

description: Baseline reset attempt failed immediately because ./autoresearch.sh was not executable on this machine (exit 126: Permission denied).

asi.hypothesis: Establish a fresh solver3 objective-quality baseline on the new machine using the current kept record-to-record code.
asi.rollback_reason: Baseline run could not start because the entrypoint lacked execute permission on this machine.
asi.next_action_hint: Mark autoresearch.sh executable or invoke it via bash, then rerun the baseline so the new-machine noise floor is recorded.

description: Baseline reset still failed: the root wrapper now ran, but its delegated tools/autoresearch/solver3-objective-quality/autoresearch.sh script is also non-executable on this machine.

asi.hypothesis: Rerun the new-machine baseline via bash wrapper after fixing the top-level entrypoint assumption.
asi.rollback_reason: The delegated solver3 autoresearch wrapper inside tools/autoresearch/solver3-objective-quality/ also lacked execute permission, so the baseline still could not start.
asi.next_action_hint: Repair execute permissions for the nested autoresearch wrapper (or invoke the nested script via bash as well), then rerun the baseline.

description: New-machine baseline run completed and produced valid metrics, but the run must be discarded because autoresearch.checks.sh delegated into a non-executable tools/autoresearch/solver3-objective-quality/autoresearch.checks.sh wrapper on this machine.

asi.hypothesis: Record a fresh current-code baseline on the new machine before continuing solver3 search experiments.
asi.rollback_reason: The benchmark itself passed, but the required post-run checks could not execute because the delegated checks wrapper lacked execute permission on this machine.
asi.next_action_hint: Mark both autoresearch checks wrappers executable, rerun the same baseline, then start new search experiments from that clean new-machine segment.
asi.machine_reset: User explicitly requested a new baseline because this machine differs from the previous setup.
observations: The current kept record-to-record solver landed at objective_suite_weighted_normalized_score=102.06579338872064 on this machine.
This is materially slower than the previous machine: solver3_raw_score_us=79.59, with path and representative diagnostics also higher.
Quality shape stayed plausible: sailing and social_golfer beat reference, while attribute_balance, large_gender, and synthetic regressed.

description: Fresh solver3 objective-quality baseline on the new machine after fixing missing execute bits on the autoresearch wrapper scripts. Current kept record-to-record baseline is 101.3513 here; slower machine profile shows solver3_raw_score_us 77.30 with strongest gains in sailing/social_golfer and biggest gaps in attribute_balance, large_gender, and synthetic.

asi.hypothesis: Reset the autoresearch lane onto a clean new-machine baseline before further solver3 search changes.
asi.machine_reset: User explicitly requested a new baseline because this machine differs from the previous setup.
asi.environment_fix: Restored executable permissions on autoresearch wrapper/check scripts so the benchmark/check pipeline can run normally on this machine.
asi.baseline_observation: New-machine baseline is 101.35127940997147, materially worse than prior-machine history, so old noise/confidence should not be reused.

description: Front-loaded elite-scout multi-start crashed during the canonical suite: benchmark aggregation reported `case stretch.sailing-trip-demo-real has no final_score`, so at least one solver3 run failed to emit a valid completed case result under the new scouting orchestration.

asi.hypothesis: Use a short elite-scout phase (current state + three diverse seeded starts) to pick a better basin, then spend the remaining fixed-time budget on the standard record-to-record search.
asi.rollback_reason: The suite aborted because sailing-trip-demo-real produced no final_score after the new scouting orchestration, indicating a hard integration bug rather than a fair quality regression.
asi.next_action_hint: Inspect the failing suite log/report for sailing-trip-demo-real; likely causes are zero/invalid remaining time budget handling, missing result emission after the scout phase, or a solver error triggered by seeded reinitialization.
asi.mechanism_family: smarter elite scouting / concentrated multi-start portfolio

description: Noise-floor rerun on the new-machine baseline: slightly worse than the fresh baseline (101.3513→101.6588), so discard. This confirms the lane now has visible machine variance, with especially noisy raw-runtime/path diagnostics (solver3_raw_score_us 77.30→99.86, path_allowed_sessions_iter_us 25.15→81.84) even while objective quality stayed within ~0.31 points.

asi.hypothesis: Re-running the unchanged post-reset baseline will establish a new-machine noise floor before more ambitious solver3 changes.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric did not improve; the rerun was slightly worse and is best treated as variance measurement rather than a keep.
asi.next_action_hint: Use ~0.3 objective-score movement as an initial rough noise band on this machine and prefer experiments that target larger shifts or show consistent per-case wins; next real code experiment should be a lower-risk structural change than the crashed elite-scout orchestration.
observations: Per-case shape was almost unchanged versus the kept baseline.
Raw runtime diagnostics were much noisier than objective quality on this machine.
This supports doing at least occasional confirmation reruns before keeping marginal improvements.

description: Front-loaded elite-scout constructor portfolio with honest time accounting regressed catastrophically on the canonical bundle. Even after fail-soft skipping invalid diversified seeds, sailing exploded to 1.837x reference and large_gender worsened to 1.101x, so this light scouting form is not a viable next step.

asi.hypothesis: Spend a small front-loaded portion of the fixed-time budget on several short seeded scout runs, keep the best scout incumbent, then spend the remaining honest budget on the standard record-to-record search.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric collapsed from the new-machine baseline 101.3513 to 116.5707, dominated by a severe sailing failure and worse large_gender.
asi.next_action_hint: Retire light constructor-portfolio scouting for now. Pivot to a different top-queue family that changes the search mechanics more fundamentally, likely targeted ALNS/LNS repair or a stronger constructor rather than more seeded scouting.
asi.sanity_bug_learned: Some diversified constructor seeds are invalid on sailing due clique-capacity normalization; fail-soft skipping avoids crashes but does not rescue the search family.

description: Targeted two-group exact repair kernel was benchmark-honest but essentially inert. The occasional stagnation-triggered restricted repair over a session/group-pair subproblem left the aggregate basically unchanged versus the new-machine baseline (101.3513→101.3785), with small case reshuffling only.

asi.hypothesis: A tiny targeted ALNS/LNS step—exact repartition over a stagnated two-group subproblem with immovables fixed and full oracle rescoring—would unlock local improvements the move-by-move search misses.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric did not improve and the delta (+0.0272) is well inside the new-machine noise band, so this exact-repair micro form is too weak to justify keeping.
asi.next_action_hint: Retire this tiny two-group restricted repair form, but keep the broader ALNS/LNS family alive. Next try should use a larger hotspot destroy set or pivot to the still-untried top-queue memetic/GRASP families rather than more micro repair tuning.
observations: Sailing stayed good (0.9687x), but large_gender and synthetic remained the same weak spots as baseline.
The mechanism appears to have had little or no practical activation/coverage under the current trigger and neighborhood size.
This is a family-level signal that the destroy/repair region must be larger or better targeted to matter.

description: Structural tabu keyed to session/group-pair corridors was catastrophically bad. The coarse memory blocked too much legitimate revisitation, blowing up sailing to 1.875x reference, regressing social_golfer, and making representative/path hotpaths unusably slow.

asi.hypothesis: A coarse structural tabu over session/group-pair churn would suppress local corridor cycling better than exact-undo tabu and help the record-to-record search escape repeated structural loops.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary collapsed from the new-machine baseline 101.3513 to 118.3136, with major quality damage and extreme raw-runtime/path regressions.
asi.next_action_hint: Retire coarse group-pair structural tabu. The remaining strongest untried paths are now the more architectural ones: elite-population memetic search, a genuinely stronger GRASP-style constructor, or macro-operator hyper-heuristics—not more blanket memory bans.

description: First memetic-style follow-up on the new machine: added a one-shot stagnation-triggered offspring burst that briefly polishes a donor from a diversified seed, then transplants the donor's best repeat-pressure session into the incumbent before resuming record-to-record search. Primary improved slightly (101.3513→101.2810), but the gain is small and should be confirmed with a rerun because it is still within the current machine noise band.

asi.hypothesis: A lightweight memetic offspring burst using an elite incumbent recipient and a briefly polished diversified donor can improve search basin quality without the catastrophic budget fragmentation of full front-loaded multi-start.
asi.machine_noise_context: Current rough noise band on this machine is ~0.3 objective-score points, so this first gain is promising but not yet conclusive.
asi.mechanism_family: memetic / elite polished donor session transplant
asi.structural_change: During prolonged stagnation, generate one donor via short inner search, pick the donor session with the best local repeat-pressure improvement, transplant it into the incumbent, then continue the main search honestly within the same wall-clock budget.
asi.next_action_hint: Immediately rerun the same committed memetic-burst code once or twice to test whether the small primary improvement survives noise before incubating further.

description: Confirmation rerun of the memetic donor-session burst did not reproduce the slight gain from the first run. Same committed code landed at 101.4529 vs the kept 101.2810 and baseline 101.3513, so the current local-repeat-pressure donor-session selector looks too noisy to trust.

asi.hypothesis: Re-running the committed memetic-burst code will show whether the initial 101.2810 improvement is repeatable on this machine.
asi.rollback_reason: The confirmation rerun regressed relative to the memetic keep and also landed above the baseline, so the current session-selection rule is not yet robust.
asi.next_action_hint: Stay within the memetic incubation window but strengthen compatibility selection: score all candidate donor-session transplants by actual offspring score (or a richer compatibility heuristic) instead of choosing by local repeat-pressure gain alone.
asi.mechanism_family: memetic / elite polished donor session transplant

description: Memetic follow-up inside the incubation window: replaced the donor-session selector's cheap local repeat-pressure proxy with exact offspring rescoring across all allowable donor-session transplants, then accepted the best offspring under the existing record-to-record gate. This improved the primary metric from 101.2810 to 101.0558, with better transfer-balance and slightly better sailing while holding social_golfer and large_gender flat versus the prior memetic keep.

asi.hypothesis: A memetic donor burst becomes more robust if donor-session compatibility is evaluated by actual offspring score rather than a noisy local repeat-pressure proxy.
asi.mechanism_family: memetic / elite polished donor session transplant
asi.structural_change: During stagnation, polish one diversified donor briefly, enumerate all allowed one-session donor transplants into the incumbent, fully rescore each offspring, and inject only the best-scoring offspring back into the main record-to-record search.
asi.next_action_hint: Confirm this stronger memetic selector with at least one rerun; if it holds, continue incubation by testing richer donor compatibility or two-session offspring only if they remain benchmark-honest within the same time budget.

description: Confirmation rerun of the exact-rescored memetic donor-session burst stayed directionally positive versus the old baselines but did not beat the current memetic keep. The rerun landed at 101.1196, which supports that the stronger selector is materially better than the earlier proxy-based variant, but the best observed result remains 101.0558.

asi.hypothesis: The exact-rescored donor-session memetic burst should reproduce as a real improvement family rather than a one-off noise artifact.
asi.rollback_reason: The rerun was worse than the current keep (101.0558), so there is no new primary improvement to keep from this cycle.
asi.next_action_hint: Retain the kept exact-rescored memetic selector and use the next incubation turn on a richer but still budget-honest compatibility follow-up, or switch to the next untried large family if memetic follow-ups start plateauing.

description: Memetic follow-up that greedily allowed up to two exact-rescored donor-session transplants in one burst regressed badly. The second transplanted session appears to overfit the donor structure and especially hurt transfer-balance, so the current exact-rescored one-session offspring remains the better kept shape.

asi.hypothesis: A budget-honest greedy second donor-session transplant could unlock a stronger memetic offspring than the kept one-session exact-rescored burst.
asi.rollback_reason: Allowing a second transplanted donor session materially worsened the primary metric, mainly via transfer-balance regression, so this richer offspring shape is not worth keeping in its current form.
asi.next_action_hint: Retire naive greedy two-session extension inside this memetic line; if memetic incubation continues, prefer better donor compatibility or multi-donor choice rather than stacking extra donor sessions onto one offspring.

description: Memetic donor-choice follow-up: split the donor-polish burst across two diversified donors, exactly rescored all allowable one-session donor transplants for each donor, and injected the best offspring under the existing record-to-record gate. This improved the primary metric from 101.0558 to 100.8462, mainly via better transfer-balance and sailing while holding social_golfer and large_gender flat.

asi.hypothesis: A small diversified donor set is better than a single donor in the memetic burst because donor choice matters more than extra polish on one donor.
asi.structural_change: When stagnation triggers a memetic burst, spend the same total donor budget across two diversified polished donors, evaluate exact one-session offspring from each donor, and inject only the globally best offspring back into the main record-to-record search.
asi.next_action_hint: Confirm the multi-donor memetic keep with at least one rerun; if it holds, further incubation should prefer smarter donor diversity/compatibility over larger offspring stacks.

description: Confirmation rerun of the two-donor memetic burst did not reproduce the 100.8462 keep. The rerun regressed to 101.6589, mainly through worse transfer-balance and large_gender, so the stronger donor-choice mechanism still looks promising but remains noisy and not yet stable enough to extrapolate from one best run alone.

asi.hypothesis: The two-donor exact-rescored memetic burst will confirm as a robust improvement over the one-donor version.
asi.rollback_reason: The confirmation rerun was substantially worse than the current keep and even worse than the old baseline, so there is no new improvement to keep from this cycle.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep the current best two-donor memetic result as the incumbent, but if incubation continues prefer stability-oriented donor diversity or compatibility controls before increasing donor count further.

description: Memetic stability follow-up: ranked each donor by the average of its top two exact-rescored one-session offspring scores, then chose the best session from the most robust-looking donor instead of taking the globally luckiest single donor-session offspring. This did not beat the current keep, landing at 101.2241, so the simple top-two donor-robustness heuristic did not stabilize the noisy two-donor burst.

asi.hypothesis: Two-donor memetic search will stabilize if donor choice rewards robustness across multiple strong session transplants rather than one lucky best session.
asi.rollback_reason: The donor-robustness ranking regressed relative to the current keep and offered no new primary improvement.
asi.next_action_hint: Retain the current best two-donor memetic keep; if memetic incubation continues, prefer compatibility features tied to structure or case pressure rather than generic top-two donor averaging.

description: Memetic post-crossover intensification follow-up: after selecting the best exact-rescored donor-session offspring from the two-donor burst, ran a short session-restricted polish on the transplanted session before returning to the main search. This improved social_golfer materially, but the aggregate still regressed to 101.1237 because transfer-balance and large_gender did not improve enough.

asi.hypothesis: A short session-local polish immediately after donor-session transplantation will repair crossover seams and stabilize the two-donor memetic burst.
asi.rollback_reason: The post-crossover single-session polish did not beat the current keep; social_golfer improved, but the aggregate remained worse than 100.8462.
asi.next_action_hint: Retain the two-donor keep and stop spending turns on simple memetic stabilizers if they continue to miss; the next fresh experiment should likely pivot to the next untried large family rather than layering more local polish.
asi.mechanism_family: memetic / multi-donor exact-rescored offspring plus session-local polish

description: First freedom-aware GRASP-style constructor trial: after the shared baseline construction, rebuilt every session with a randomized restricted-candidate greedy pass that respected cliques/immovables, scored candidate groups using repeat/contact deltas plus attribute-balance/constraint heuristics, and fed the resulting schedule into the kept record-to-record search. It improved synthetic and sailing and held social_golfer near reference, but the aggregate still missed the current memetic keep because transfer-balance and large_gender remained too weak.

asi.hypothesis: A solver3-local freedom-aware GRASP rebuild of the initial schedule can produce stronger basins for the kept record-to-record search than the shared baseline constructor alone.
asi.rollback_reason: The first GRASP-style constructor pass did not beat the current best 100.8462 primary score, so it cannot be kept yet.
asi.next_action_hint: Move the GRASP family out of the untried queue and give it an incubation follow-up that specifically strengthens balance-heavy cases (attribute_balance/large_gender) without losing the synthetic and sailing gains.

description: First stronger targeted ALNS/LNS follow-up on the new machine: added a one-shot stagnation-triggered exact repair burst that selected a hotspot session, freed three hotspot groups, repartitioned the affected clique/person units across those groups with an exact restricted search, and resumed the kept record-to-record search from the best repaired state. This was benchmark-honest and safe, but it did not improve the aggregate: it landed at 101.4627, effectively showing that this simple hotspot selector and small exact kernel are still too weak in this form.

asi.hypothesis: A larger but still restricted exact repair burst over three hotspot groups can make the ALNS/LNS family materially stronger than the previously inert two-group exact kernel.
asi.rollback_reason: The first three-group exact repair burst regressed versus the current keep and did not show enough directional lift to justify keeping this implementation.
asi.next_action_hint: Move this ALNS/LNS item out of the untried queue; if the family is revisited later, it likely needs better destroy targeting, richer hotspot structure, or a stronger mixed repair operator rather than just a slightly larger exact partition kernel.
asi.mechanism_family: targeted ALNS/LNS with restricted exact repair burst

description: First stage-aware macro-operator follow-up: replaced the fixed memetic burst choice with a simple stage-aware controller that switched between standard memetic exploitation and a balance-focused memetic burst based on progress and balance/constraint gaps, while keeping the same benchmark-honest donor mechanics underneath. This did not improve the aggregate; it regressed to 101.3645, mainly because large_gender worsened while the balance-focused selector did not produce enough compensating gains elsewhere.

asi.hypothesis: A stage-aware controller over macro operators can improve robustness by invoking a balance-focused burst later in the search instead of always using the same memetic exploitation operator.
asi.rollback_reason: The first stage-aware macro-operator controller regressed versus the current keep and did not show evidence that this simple context rule improves operator choice.
asi.next_action_hint: Move the hyper-heuristic item out of the untried queue; if revisiting this family later, use richer operator diversity or learned/contextual rewards rather than a simple deterministic balance-vs-memetic rule over nearly identical operators.
asi.mechanism_family: stage-aware macro-operator controller over memetic burst variants

description: First structural-compatibility follow-up: added a lightweight session-level compatibility layer to the memetic burst that scored donor sessions by structural improvement (repeat-pressure plus attribute-balance pressure, with a shape-distance penalty) and only exactly rescored the top structurally promising transplants. This did not improve the aggregate: it landed at 101.3924, suggesting this simple session-signature filter is too weak and may be over-pruning useful donor-session options.

asi.hypothesis: A structural-compatibility layer that prefilters donor-session transplants by session pressure improvement and shape similarity can stabilize the noisy memetic donor-choice line.
asi.rollback_reason: The structural compatibility prefilter regressed versus the current keep and did not show enough value to justify keeping this first implementation.
asi.next_action_hint: Move the hypergraph/compatibility item out of the untried queue; if revisited later, use richer structural signatures or compatibility features than simple session pressure plus shape distance.
asi.mechanism_family: structural-compatibility layer for memetic donor-session selection

description: Second freedom-aware GRASP incubation follow-up: kept the solver3-local constructor repair idea but focused it on the top attribute-pressure sessions only, rebuilding those sessions with a balance-heavy greedy/RCL pass after baseline construction. This shifted quality toward sailing and synthetic, but the aggregate regressed badly because transfer-balance and large_gender worsened, so the current balance-focused constructor follow-up is not worth keeping.

asi.hypothesis: A balance-focused GRASP rebuild on only the top attribute-pressure sessions will preserve the first GRASP variant's synthetic gains while improving the balance-heavy cases that blocked it.
asi.rollback_reason: The focused balance-rebuild follow-up regressed to 101.6319 and worsened large_gender materially, so this follow-up is not a keep.
asi.next_action_hint: The GRASP family may need a different balance signal or selective partial rebuild to avoid harming large_gender; otherwise shift back to the still-best memetic family or synthesize a genuinely new architecture.

description: Population-lite memetic archive follow-up: added an in-run elite archive intended to reuse diverse high-quality states discovered during the same search as additional donors for the stagnation-triggered memetic burst. On this first implementation the benchmark matched the earlier two-donor baseline-like behavior exactly, implying the archive was not accumulating materially distinct elite donors before the burst and therefore contributed no useful new recombination power.

asi.hypothesis: A lightweight elite archive of diverse within-run high-quality states can make the memetic burst more like a real population search and improve donor quality without extra constructor fragmentation.
asi.rollback_reason: The first elite-archive implementation produced no primary improvement and appears effectively inert in practice, so there is nothing to keep from this cycle.
asi.next_action_hint: If continuing this population-lite direction, make archive insertion much more aggressive or structurally aware so the burst actually has distinct donor states to choose from; otherwise pivot to a different substantial idea.

failure_shape.symptom: archive likely contained no materially distinct donor states by burst time

description: Second population-lite memetic archive attempt: relaxed elite insertion and donor eligibility so the stagnation burst could reuse near-best, structurally different states from the same run. This did move the outcome off the exact baseline-like plateau, but still failed clearly versus the kept two-donor memetic line; gains stayed concentrated in sailing/social_golfer while large_gender and synthetic remained the blockers.

asi.hypothesis: A more permissive within-run elite archive, with periodic snapshots and score slack, can supply genuinely distinct donors to the memetic burst and approximate a small population without paying for full multi-start fragmentation.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary quality remained well above the kept memetic best (101.2664 vs 100.8462), so the population-lite archive is still not competitive in its current form.
asi.next_action_hint: Record this as a materially tried archive/population variant and either pivot to a deeper population mechanism with explicit survivor management/recombination timing or move to a different large-family idea; do not keep iterating cheap archive-threshold tweaks.
asi.learning: More aggressive archive admission made the mechanism active but did not solve the core donor-quality/compatibility problem.

description: Survivor-managed donor-pool memetic follow-up: allowed two stagnation-triggered bursts, stored polished donors and accepted offspring in a small pool, and reused structurally distinct pool donors in later bursts. This deeper population-lite step still failed to beat the kept two-donor memetic line and ended up nearly identical to the prior relaxed archive attempt, suggesting the extra survivor machinery still was not creating meaningfully better donor material.

asi.hypothesis: A small explicit donor pool carried across multiple memetic bursts can act like a true population and improve donor reuse beyond passive archive snapshots.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary quality again stayed far above the kept memetic best (101.2665 vs 100.8462), with essentially the same case pattern as the relaxed archive follow-up.
asi.next_action_hint: Retire this lightweight survivor-managed population variant in its current form; if memetic work continues, it likely needs stronger donor generation or a richer recombination unit than one-session exact transplant.
asi.learning: Adding multiple bursts and simple survivor rules did not materially change donor quality; the bottleneck now looks more like recombination power or donor-construction quality than archive persistence.

description: Exact joint two-session donor recombination: replaced single-session exact offspring scoring with exact evaluation of every 1-session and 2-session transplant bundle from each polished donor. This benchmark-honest richer recombination unit still regressed versus the kept two-donor single-session memetic line and also slowed the hotpath, so the extra bundle expressiveness did not unlock the missing quality.

asi.hypothesis: Exact evaluation of joint two-session donor bundles can capture positive interaction that naive greedy stacking missed and make memetic recombination meaningfully stronger.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary remained well above the kept memetic best (101.2944 vs 100.8462) and solver3_raw_score_us also worsened materially.
asi.next_action_hint: Record this as the first materially tried richer recombination unit; further memetic work should probably focus on much stronger donor generation or a different macro neighborhood rather than more exhaustive bundle scoring over the same donor source.
asi.learning: The bottleneck now appears less about greedy interaction misspecification and more about the donor source/recombination unit itself.

description: Incumbent-derived hotspot donor generation: instead of polishing fully fresh donors, each memetic donor started from the incumbent best state, replaced the top repeat/balance-pressure sessions with sessions from a diversified random donor, then polished and competed via exact one-session transplant. This new perturb-and-repair donor source was benchmark-honest and directionally active, but still not competitive with the kept two-donor memetic line.

asi.hypothesis: Memetic donors that start from the incumbent and are perturbed through hotspot-session replacement will be structurally more compatible than fully fresh donors, producing higher-value offspring after donor polishing.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary quality remained clearly above the kept memetic best (101.2650 vs 100.8462), so the first hotspot-donor generator is not yet good enough to keep.
asi.next_action_hint: Within this incubation window, try selecting replacement sessions by donor-vs-incumbent structural advantage rather than just incumbent hotspot pressure; if that still stalls, retire this donor-generation variant.
asi.learning: Changing donor generation was more meaningful than archive/pool tweaks, but a pure incumbent-hotspot selector still imports the wrong sessions too often.

description: Incubation follow-up on incumbent-derived donor generation: instead of replacing the incumbent's hottest sessions, the donor generator imported only sessions where a diversified donor looked structurally better on repeat/balance pressure before donor polishing. This sharper donor-advantage selector overfit the local structural proxy and regressed the primary aggregate despite strong social_golfer gains.

asi.hypothesis: Importing only sessions where the randomized donor is structurally better than the incumbent will produce higher-quality perturbed donors than blindly mutating incumbent hotspot sessions.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary quality regressed to 101.3868 and the selector worsened transfer-balance, large_gender, and synthetic despite better social_golfer.
asi.next_action_hint: Pause this donor-generation incubation and rerun the kept champion to measure whether recent regressions are just machine drift/noise or a real thermal/system effect, as requested by the user.
asi.learning: A simple donor-vs-incumbent session-pressure proxy is too myopic; it imports sessions that look locally attractive but do not improve global offspring quality.

description: Champion rerun on unchanged kept commit 903743d to check stability/thermal drift. The same code landed well above its kept best (100.8462 -> 101.3785), with materially slower raw runtime diagnostics, so the champion did not hold on this rerun and the machine appears slower than at the winning run.

asi.hypothesis: If the champion still performs near 100.8462 with similar runtime diagnostics, the earlier win likely held; if both quality and raw runtime regress together, machine-state drift such as thermal slowdown is a plausible contributor.
asi.rollback_reason: This was a diagnostic rerun on unchanged code and did not improve the primary metric.
asi.next_action_hint: Run at least one more unchanged-champion rerun to separate stochastic quality noise from sustained machine slowdown; compare solver3_raw_score_us and path/hotpath probes across reruns.
asi.learning: Same-code quality regression coincided with slower raw runtime, which is more consistent with system-state drift than with a genuine code regression.

description: Second unchanged-champion rerun to check stability after the first slow diagnostic rerun. The result stayed poor relative to the kept best and remained far above 100.8462, so the earlier champion win still stands; the issue is that current reruns are underperforming, not that recent experimental variants secretly beat the champion.

asi.hypothesis: A second same-code rerun will tell us whether the first bad rerun was a one-off random miss or part of a sustained worse regime.
asi.rollback_reason: This was another diagnostic rerun on unchanged code and again did not improve the primary metric.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not reinterpret the bad reruns as evidence that newer discarded variants were better; instead treat them as evidence of substantial same-code variance/system-state drift and compare all recent results against the actual champion value 100.8462.
asi.learning: Back-to-back same-code reruns remained ~0.53-0.56 worse than the kept best, so the champion did not reproduce, but neither rerun crossed below the champion.

description: Hotspot repair burst follow-up: added a stagnation-triggered clique-aware macro repair operator that picks the highest-pressure session and applies up to three exact-recomputed improving block transfers (singletons or whole active cliques) before the existing memetic burst. This stronger session-local repair idea was benchmark-honest but clearly uncompetitive, worsening transfer-balance without fixing the large_gender/synthetic blockers.

asi.hypothesis: A targeted clique-safe hotspot repair burst over exact block transfers within one pressured session can give ALNS-style repair coverage that is broader than the old tiny two-group kernel while remaining compatible with the current search loop.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary regressed badly to 101.7291, so this first session-local block-transfer repair burst is not competitive enough to keep.
asi.next_action_hint: Record this as a materially tried macro repair operator and pivot again; if repair is revisited later, it likely needs richer destroy+repair structure or swap/cycle coverage rather than greedy exact block transfers alone.
asi.learning: Even clique-aware exact block transfers inside one hotspot session are too myopic in this form and can make the balance case worse.

description: Cross-donor mixed-session memetic recombination: after polishing the two diversified donors, exactly rescored not only the usual one-session transplants but also all two-session offspring that mix one session from donor A with a different session from donor B. This richer cross-donor bundle was more competitive than several recent follow-ups and improved sailing/large_gender versus the recent diagnostic reruns, but it still did not beat the kept champion.

asi.hypothesis: The two polished donors may contain complementary good sessions, so exact mixed two-session bundles across different donors can unlock recombination value that same-donor bundles and simple donor choice miss.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary improved relative to many recent follow-ups but still stayed above the kept best (101.2038 vs 100.8462), so the cross-donor mixed bundle is not yet good enough to keep.
asi.next_action_hint: Record this as a materially tried richer memetic recombination variant; if continuing the memetic line, look for stronger donor-generation or more global compatibility signals rather than more exhaustive session-bundle enumeration.
asi.learning: Cross-donor complementarity is directionally better than same-donor bundle enumeration, but the gain is still too small and the chronic transfer/synthetic blockers remain.

description: Cross-donor child-polish memetic recombination: after polishing the two diversified donors, built mixed donor children from each donor's top exact-rescored session candidates, polished those children briefly, then rescored their best one-session offspring against the incumbent. This deeper recombine-then-local-search variant was benchmark-honest but clearly regressed, especially on transfer-balance and synthetic, so the extra donor-child polishing did not unlock useful complementarities.

asi.hypothesis: A mixed donor child that inherits strong sessions from both polished donors and is then locally polished can expose useful cross-donor complementarity that direct offspring bundle enumeration misses.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary regressed badly to 101.9771 and added overhead, so this child-polish recombination variant is not competitive.
asi.next_action_hint: Record this as a materially tried deeper memetic recombination variant and stop spending turns on richer session-bundle recombination over the current donor source; the next step should be a genuinely different macro idea or a much stronger donor-generation concept.
asi.learning: Adding a recombine-then-polish child on top of the current donor source made the memetic family slower and worse, suggesting the donor material itself is the limiting factor rather than insufficient local optimization after recombination.

description: Two-stage donor scouting memetic follow-up: expanded the burst into a donor portfolio that briefly polished four scout donors, ranked them by exact best-offspring potential, then spent the remaining donor budget polishing only the top two before normal exact session-transplant evaluation. This benchmark-honest elite-scout donor strategy was clearly worse, especially on transfer-balance, so donor budget fragmentation inside the memetic burst did not improve donor quality.

asi.hypothesis: A scout-then-intensify donor portfolio can find better memetic donors than two fixed diversified seeds without paying the full cost of naive multi-start across the whole run.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary regressed badly to 102.2405 and worsened runtime diagnostics, so this donor-scout portfolio is not competitive.
asi.next_action_hint: Record this as a materially tried donor-portfolio variant and stop spending turns on donor-budget fragmentation inside the current memetic burst; the next idea should be a different mechanism family or a fundamentally different donor source.
asi.learning: Even a benchmark-honest scout-then-intensify donor portfolio behaves like harmful budget fragmentation here; better donor selection does not emerge from this light portfolio approach.

description: Session-global exact person-reassignment repair: added a stagnation-triggered restricted exact repair that picked the highest attribute-pressure session, selected up to four movable non-clique people contributing surplus attribute mass, enumerated compact cross-group reassignment combinations, and accepted the best exact-oracle-improving repair before the memetic burst. This was a stronger cross-group exact repair than the old two-group kernel and less catastrophic than the block-transfer burst, but it still failed to improve the chronic transfer/large-gender/synthetic blockers enough to challenge the incumbent.

asi.hypothesis: A stronger restricted exact repair over a small set of imbalance-causing people, with reassignment freedom across the whole session rather than just one group-pair, can address the large_gender/transfer-balance weakness more directly than earlier local repair attempts.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary remained materially above the current incumbent (101.6310 vs 100.8462), so this exact person-reassignment repair is not competitive in its first form.
asi.next_action_hint: Record this as a materially tried stronger ALNS/LNS repair variant; if repair is revisited later, expand destroy selection or allow richer swap/cycle structure rather than only direct reassignment of a few imbalance-contributing singles.
asi.learning: Widening the exact repair from one group-pair to a small whole-session reassignment set was directionally more meaningful than the tiny kernel, but direct reassignment alone still cannot overcome the chronic objective blockers.

description: Baseline for the new release-mode solver3 metaheuristic autoresearch lane on the 150s/7M mixed feature-surface bundle.

Tried capability-aware suite splitting that routed repeat-encounter stretch cases through single_state + sgp_week_pair_tabu while keeping feature-rich stretch cases on baseline modes; Social Golfer improved but large_gender regressed enough to make the mixed 150s/7M bundle worse overall.
Crash: autoresearch wrapper lost execute permission after discard/revert, so ./autoresearch.sh failed immediately with permission denied before the zero-repeat-only routing experiment could run.
